description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular form and a prominent cathode ray tube (CRT) display. The front features a large, dark screen flanked by a vertical row of physical buttons and a red power indicator, typical of older TV models. On top, two metal V-shaped antenna rods extend upward, suggesting analog signal reception. The sides and back are adorned with geometric patterns and ventilation grilles, providing both structural detail and necessary airflow for internal electronics. The casing appears to be made of dark plastic or metal, with a matte finish and subtle textural variations. This type of television would commonly be found in urban homes, garages, or even discarded on sidewalks.
description: A vintage CRT television with a boxy shape, large screen, physical buttons, and metal antenna rods on top. The casing is dark and matte, with ventilation grilles and geometric detailing.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the ventilation grille and geometric patterns, with antenna rods visible on top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the casing and the protruding back section, with antenna rods extending upward.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the large CRT screen, vertical row of buttons, and a red power indicator, with antenna rods on top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the boxy profile and antenna rods.
